name: Montana Educators'
description_short: Montana Educators' Credit Union is an NCUA-insured credit union based in Missoula serving educators with savings accounts, CDs, checking, and loan products.
description_long: Montana Educators' Credit Union was established in 1995 and is headquartered in Missoula, Montana. As a state-chartered, NCUA-insured credit union, it operates under charter number 68190 and serves members in the education sector. The institution has grown to manage approximately $32.94 million in assets, $29.59 million in deposits, and $24.26 million in loans, with a capital base of $3.15 million. The credit union offers a comprehensive range of deposit and loan products. On the deposit side, members can access savings accounts, money market accounts, and CDs ranging from 6 months to 24 months in term length. Interest checking accounts are also available. For borrowing needs, Montana Educators' provides mortgages (30-year and 15-year fixed options), home equity lines of credit, auto loans, personal loans, and home equity loans. Current deposit rates are modest, with savings accounts at 0.05% APY and 12-month CDs at 0.25% APY as of April 2026. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Montana Educators' operates on a member-ownership model, which distinguishes it from commercial banks. The institution maintains physical branch locations in Montana and serves a membership base tied to the education profession. The credit union is relatively small compared to national financial institutions, which may limit the breadth of digital capabilities and national reach compared to larger competitors. Montana Educators' Credit Union is best suited for educators and education-sector employees in Montana seeking basic banking services with a community-focused institution. However, the deposit rates are significantly below current market averages (savings at 0.05% vs. 4.20% market average; 1-year CDs at 0.25% vs. 4.59% market average), making it less attractive for rate-sensitive savers. Prospective members should consider whether membership eligibility and local service justify substantially lower yields on deposits.
pros: NCUA insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000 per account category
Established institution with 29 years of operating history since 1995
Multiple deposit products including savings, money market, and CDs with various term lengths
Offers mortgage products for both purchase and refinancing needs
Community-based credit union structure aligned with education professionals
Multiple branch locations in Montana for in-person service
Provides home equity loans and HELOCs for borrowing flexibility
cons: Savings account rates (0.05% APY) are significantly below market average (4.20%), representing 98% lower yield
CD rates substantially underperform market rates across all terms (0.25% vs 4.59% for 1-year)
Limited geographic presence confined to Montana; not available nationwide
No loan rate information currently available on website; members must contact directly
Small asset base ($32.94M) limits product innovation and digital banking capabilities
best_for: Montana-based educators and school employees seeking membership-based banking
Members prioritizing in-person branch service and community focus over competitive rates
Borrowers needing mortgage or home equity products from a local credit union
diagnosis: Montana Educators' Credit Union is best for education professionals in Montana seeking community-based banking with membership ownership benefits and local branch access. The critical caveat is that deposit rates are substantially uncompetitive (0.05% savings, 0.25% 1-year CD) versus national market averages, making this credit union primarily valuable for borrowing products or members who prioritize community service over yield optimization.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
